name: UK Garden Wildlife Photography: Birds, Bees, and Beyond

step.name: Choose your shot type and target species
step.text: Decide before session begins. One shot type — feeder/bird, pollinator, pond, or small mammal — with a specific target species. Decision drives lens, time of day, position, infrastructure. 'See what shows up' is not a plan.

step.name: Refine the setup the day before
step.text: Walk garden evening before with shot type in mind. Check perch condition and light direction. Replace tired perches. Top up feeders. Clear background distractions. Half the keeper-versus-throwaway difference decided here.

step.name: Be in position before activity starts
step.text: 20 minutes before peak activity. Dawn songbirds = 4:50am May/June. Pollinators = 9:30am. Dusk hedgehogs = 8pm. Birds need time to confirm you're not a threat.

step.name: Dial in settings while you wait
step.text: Aperture priority f/4-f/6.3 birds, f/8 macro. Auto ISO with sensible upper. Shutter min 1/500 songbirds, 1/1000 in-flight. Continuous AF on perch position. RAW. Test-shoot before subject arrives.

step.name: Shoot in burst, hold for behaviour
step.text: Short bursts of 3-5 frames when target lands. Focus on eye. Hold and wait for behaviour. Don't chase moving subjects randomly. Keeper rarely first frame — usually frame 2-3 of burst.

step.name: Cull, edit, and plan next session
step.text: Cull on import — only sharp frames with eye catch-light. 5-15 keepers from 90-minute session. Edit lightly. Note what worked for next session. Long-running practice, not one-off.
